extracted_information: Real-time collaboration algorithms aim for eventual consistency. Operational Transformation (OT) relies on a server connection to coordinate and transform operations, handling complex data structures and user intent well but being highly complex. Conflict-free Replicated Data Types (CRDTs) can work peer-to-peer and offline by breaking data into small pieces, guaranteeing data consistency but struggling with capturing user intent and advanced rich text features, often leading to compromises in user experience.

operational_transformation.mechanism: User actions broken into operations, transmitted with baseline reference. Incoming operations are transformed to include local operations that happened since that baseline. Constant transformation of operations.
operational_transformation.server_reliance: Relies on an active server connection to coordinate and guarantee correctness. Original vision did not require a server, but research showed too many edge cases without it. Server-based OT splits multi-user collaboration into client-server pairs, server coordinates document state and operation list, avoiding complex three-way transforms.
operational_transformation.correctness: Initial forms had many edge cases leading to document divergence. Only two reliable forms survived: server-based OT and OT with 'transform property 2' (TP2). TP2 is provably correct for multi-way transformation but is extremely complex to implement for data structures.

crdt.mechanism: Breaks down data into small pieces (e.g., every character as a separate entity for text). Generally doesn't need to transform the change itself, only the position of the change.
crdt.server_reliance: Capable of working peer-to-peer with end-to-end encryption. If a server is used, it only needs to coordinate connections between clients. Resilient to transient network connections, supports clients going offline and synchronizing later.
supported_data_types: Arbitrary JSON structures (using a separate plain text CRDT for each text value)

features.capability: Captures user intent: Can define complex operations like 'split text node' that are well understood in rich text editors.
features.benefit: Enables advanced structures and full rich text editing capabilities without compromising depth of features.

features.capability: Peer-to-peer and offline support: Works without a central server, resilient to network disconnections, and allows changes while offline with later synchronization.
features.benefit: Increased resilience and privacy due to end-to-end encryption capability.
